EXP-144.title: Differential Refraction: The Eclipse Geometry Trap
EXP-144.summary: n(r) medium bends ALL light regardless of source; moon physical position indeterminate; nine eclipse WINs rest on geometry the dome cannot compute
EXP-144.key_argument: At n=1.44, moonlight from r~10,000km is refracted by a glass-strength medium. Dome has no correction formula for moon. Eclipse shadow geometry requires physical positions of BOTH bodies — neither is known.

breaking_news_suggestions.headline: Dome's refraction fix makes eclipse geometry literally incomputable
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: New Section 1.9 formalizes the differential refraction trap: the dome's n(r) medium bends all light regardless of source, but the dome has never computed the moon's refraction. Since nine eclipse predictions (WIN-010, WIN-025, WIN-068 and others) require knowing both bodies' PHYSICAL positions — not apparent ones — the dome is making predictions from geometry it cannot calculate.
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: This is a structural trap, not just a numerical error. The dome cannot escape by updating parameters — the entire eclipse WIN cluster rests on uncomputable geometry as long as n(r) exists.
